key_points: Health means a state of complete physical, mental, and social well-being
A healthy lifestyle includes balanced diet, exercise, proper sleep, and hygiene
Diseases are conditions that disturb normal body functioning
Communicable diseases spread from one person to another through pathogens
Non-communicable diseases do not spread and are often related to lifestyle
Immunity is the body's ability to fight infections
Vaccines help the immune system recognize and fight pathogens
Antibiotics are medicines used to treat bacterial infections
examples: Communicable disease example: Malaria spreads through mosquito bites
Waterborne disease example: Cholera spreads through contaminated water
Vaccination example: Polio vaccine protects children from polio
important_terms: Health: Complete physical, mental, and social well-being
Pathogens: Disease-causing microorganisms such as bacteria, viruses, fungi, and protozoa
Vector: An organism that carries pathogens and spreads disease
Immunity: The body's natural defense system against diseases
Antibiotic Resistance: When bacteria become resistant to antibiotics
prevention_measures: Remove stagnant water to prevent mosquito breeding
Use antibiotics only when prescribed by a doctor
exam_tips: Remember the WHO definition of health
Understand the difference between symptoms and signs
Know the difference between communicable and non-communicable diseases
Remember examples of vector-borne and water-borne diseases
Understand why antibiotics do not work against viruses
